what_it_does: Implements a self-contained React component set (DesignCanvas, DCSection, DCArtboard, DCPostIt) that renders a warm-gray, grid-backed, pan/zoom design canvas. It groups artboards into sections, supports inline renaming, drag reordering, deletion, fullscreen focus navigation, persisted layout state, and PNG/HTML export of individual artboards.
observations: Injects dc-prefixed CSS once into document.head and guards against duplicate injection.
Uses a React context (DCCtx) to share state and mutations between canvas, sections, and artboard frames.
Persists section order, renamed titles/labels, and hidden artboards to a .design-canvas.state.json sidecar; reads via fetch and writes via window.omelette.writeFile.
Recursively flattens React.Fragment children so DCSection/DCArtboard can be discovered regardless of wrapping.
DCViewport updates transform directly on the DOM ref for pan/zoom performance and persists the viewport to localStorage keyed by pathname.
Distinguishes notched mouse wheels from trackpad scroll/pinch using delta heuristics and supports Safari gesture events.
Counter-scales chrome (headers, section titles) against viewport zoom using --dc-inv-zoom via transform and CSS zoom, with layout-drift compensation during zoom.
DCArtboard is a marker component returning null; DCArtboardFrame performs the actual rendering and interaction.
Export pipeline clones an artboard with computed styles, inlines fonts/images/backgrounds, and produces PNG via SVG foreignObject rasterized at 3x or a standalone HTML file.
Focus overlay is rendered through a ReactDOM portal to document.body and supports arrow-key navigation within and across sections.
DCPostIt renders an absolute-positioned, rotated sticky note with a comic-style font stack.
Relies on global React and ReactDOM and the host bridge window.omelette without importing them.
standouts: Hybrid persistence model: fetch for sidecar reads, window.omelette.writeFile for writes.
Self-contained export that bakes computed styles and inlines web fonts and images into PNG and HTML.
Counter-scaled UI chrome with explicit compensation for vertical layout drift caused by zoom reflow.
Drag-reorder animates sibling slots with transforms and commits DOM order only after the settle transition.
conventions: Functional React components with hooks; state managed via useState and refs.
Global CSS injection guarded by a unique style element id.
Context provider pattern for shared canvas state API.
Inline SVG icons and contentEditable elements for lightweight inline editing.
Portal for modal/focus overlay to escape ancestor transforms.
dc-prefixed class names and camelCase helper names.
Extensive inline comments documenting usage and rationale.
concerns: React and ReactDOM are assumed as globals; there is no guard if they are absent.
window.omelette is used for writes with only optional chaining fallback.
document-level keydown/pointerdown listeners in DesignCanvas and DCFocusOverlay may interfere with other contentEditable or Escape handlers.
CSS zoom in .dc-sectionhead is non-standard and browser support varies.
localStorage viewport key uses location.pathname, which could collide if multiple canvases share the same path.
Export clones the entire artboard DOM and fetches/inlines every external asset, which may be slow or memory-heavy for large canvases.
DCEditable commits on every blur, even when the value has not changed, potentially triggering unnecessary persistence writes.
The focus overlay keydown listener is registered without a dependency array, causing remove/re-add on every render.
Font CSS extraction relies on regex over fetched CSS text and may miss complex @font-face declarations or fail on cross-origin restrictions.
notes: A zero-dependency, host-integrated design canvas wrapper that adds presentation, persistence, and export chrome around generated artboard content.
questions: What is the full window.omelette API contract beyond writeFile?
How are React and ReactDOM supplied to this file in production (global UMD, bundler, etc.)?
Is the @ds-adherence-ignore directive consumed by a specific linter or build step?
What additional host message types are expected beyond __dc_zoom, __dc_set_zoom, and __dc_probe?
How are artboard children enforced to avoid height:100% and overflow scrolling as the usage comment requests?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the usage comment describes a Figma-ish canvas with sections, artboards, reordering, inline editing, focus overlay, persistence, and exports, all of which are implemented.
apparent_themes: Design-tool ergonomics inside a runtime preview
Persistence of user arrangement without mutating source artboards
intention_signals: Build a lightweight, embeddable design canvas that lets users inspect, rearrange, rename, and export generated UI variants inside a preview or host environment, while keeping the canvas chrome independent of the design content and preserving user layout across sessions.
novelty: The combination of a zero-dependency React canvas that bakes computed styles and inlines web fonts and images into both PNG (via SVG foreignObject) and HTML exports, together with counter-scaled chrome using CSS zoom and transform plus layout-drift compensation during zoom.
contribution: A complete, self-contained design-canvas runtime with pan/zoom, persisted per-section artboard order/labels/hidden state, inline editing, drag reorder, fullscreen focus overlay, and PNG/HTML export, usable as a drop-in wrapper around generated design substrates.
summary_for_embedding: A React component file implementing a Figma-like design canvas (DesignCanvas, DCSection, DCArtboard, DCPostIt) with a pan/zoom viewport, persisted user state, editable and reorderable artboards, a focus overlay, and PNG/HTML export via DOM cloning and SVG foreignObject rasterization.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a zero-dependency, host-integrated canvas for presenting and manipulating design variants with persistent layout and exportable artboards.

symbols.does: Recursively unwraps React.Fragment children so DCSection and DCArtboard can be discovered inside fragment wrappers.
symbols.description: Walks the children tree and expands any React.Fragment nodes, returning a flat array of direct child elements. This lets the canvas registry and section layout work even when sections or artboards are grouped with <>…</>.

symbols.does: Stateful wrapper that loads and saves canvas state, builds child registries, provides context, and renders the viewport plus focus overlay.
symbols.description: Reads persisted section state from .design-canvas.state.json, builds synchronous registries of sections and artboards, exposes a context API for patches and focus, attaches document-level Escape/blur handlers, and conditionally renders DCFocusOverlay for the focused artboard.

symbols.does: Document keydown listener that exits focus mode when Escape is pressed.
symbols.description: A simple effect callback registered on document that clears the focused artboard whenever the user presses Escape.

symbols.does: Document pointerdown listener that blurs an active contentEditable when clicking outside it.
symbols.description: Commits any in-progress inline rename by blurring the active element if the click target is outside it, ensuring renames are finalized on outside interaction.

symbols.does: Transform-based pan/zoom viewport with wheel, trackpad, gesture, and drag input handling.
symbols.description: Manages viewport transform in a ref, writes translate3d directly to the world element for performance, persists transform to localStorage, distinguishes mouse wheel from trackpad events, supports Safari gesture pinch, and communicates zoom to the host via postMessage.

symbols.does: Immediately saves the current viewport transform to localStorage.
symbols.description: A helper inside DCViewport that clears the debounce timer and writes tf.current to localStorage right away, used on pagehide and unmount.

symbols.does: Zooms the viewport around a screen point and compensates for layout drift from counter-scaled chrome.
symbols.description: Applies a scale factor centered on the given cursor coordinates, updates --dc-inv-zoom, then measures a DOM marker and corrects any vertical shift introduced by reflow of zoom-dependent section margins.

symbols.does: Heuristic that decides whether a wheel event came from a notched mouse wheel rather than a trackpad.
symbols.description: Returns true for line-mode deltas, integer deltas with no X component above a threshold, or other indicators of physical wheel clicks, routing those events to fixed-step zoom instead of pan.

symbols.does: Wheel event handler that routes pinch, mouse-wheel zoom, and trackpad pan behavior.
symbols.description: Prevents default scrolling, drops concurrent Safari gesture wheels, and dispatches to zoomAt for trackpad pinch, fixed-ratio zoom for notched wheels, or direct pan translation for trackpad two-finger scroll.

symbols.does: Safari gesturestart handler that begins native pinch tracking.
symbols.description: Sets a flag so concurrent wheel events are ignored, records the current scale as the gesture baseline, and prevents default to keep the browser from zooming the page.

symbols.does: Safari gesturechange handler that applies smooth pinch scale updates.
symbols.description: Uses the cumulative e.scale relative to the gesture baseline to zoom around the event point, giving smoother pinch behavior on Safari than the ctrl-wheel fallback.

symbols.does: Safari gestureend handler that ends native pinch tracking.
symbols.description: Clears the gesturing flag and prevents default to finish the Safari pinch gesture cleanly.

symbols.does: Starts drag-pan on middle button or primary button over the canvas background.
symbols.description: Captures the pointer and initializes drag state when the user middle-clicks anywhere or left-clicks on non-artboard, non-editable background, setting the viewport cursor to grabbing.

symbols.does: Updates the viewport translation during drag-pan.
symbols.description: Applies pointer deltas to the transform ref and calls apply on each move event so panning stays smooth and avoids React re-renders.

symbols.does: Ends drag-pan and restores the viewport cursor.
symbols.description: Releases pointer capture, clears drag state, and removes the grabbing cursor when the pointer is released or canceled.

symbols.does: Handles postMessage commands from the host and re-announces canvas presence on probe.
symbols.description: Responds to __dc_set_zoom by zooming around the viewport center, responds to __dc_probe by re-posting __dc_present and forcing a zoom announcement, keeping the host toolbar in sync.

symbols.does: Renders an editable section title and a flex row of artboards in persisted order.
symbols.description: Reads persisted title and order from context, computes a stable source key from artboard ids, filters hidden artboards, and renders DCArtboardFrame instances with callbacks for rename, reorder, delete, and focus.

symbols.does: Marker component that returns null; actual rendering is performed by DCArtboardFrame.
symbols.description: Serves only as a JSX tag for declaring artboards inside DCSection; it carries props such as id, label, width, height, and children, which DCSection and DCArtboardFrame consume.

symbols.does: Async export pipeline that clones an artboard and produces a self-contained PNG or HTML file.
symbols.description: Waits for fonts, collects and inlines @font-face rules, recursively clones the artboard node with computed styles, inlines image and background URLs, then either serializes to a standalone HTML document or rasterizes via SVG foreignObject to a 3x PNG download.

symbols.does: Fetches a URL and returns it as a data URI via FileReader.
symbols.description: Helper used during export to convert external image, font, or background URLs into base64 data URIs so the exported artifact has no external dependencies.

symbols.does: Recursively fetches a CSS file and extracts @font-face blocks and @import URLs.
symbols.description: Used when a stylesheet is cross-origin or otherwise inaccessible via cssRules; it downloads the CSS text, regex-extracts @font-face rules, and follows @import chains to collect nested font declarations.

symbols.does: Recursively walks CSSRule lists to collect @font-face rules and handle nested imports/media.
symbols.description: Iterates over a stylesheet's rules, recording FONT_FACE_RULE cssText, descending into IMPORT_RULE styleSheets, and recursing into cssRules inside @media or @supports blocks.

symbols.does: Constructs the inlined @font-face CSS string by converting relative font URLs to data URIs.
symbols.description: Inside dcExport, maps the collected @font-face rules into a single CSS string where each url(...) reference is replaced by its data URI, ensuring exported HTML/PNG carry their own fonts.

symbols.does: Recursively clones a DOM node while baking computed styles and disabling animations.
symbols.description: Creates a deep clone of the source node, copies every computed style property into an inline style attribute, strips scripts and comments, converts canvas elements to images, and disables transitions/animations for a clean export.

symbols.does: Triggers a download of a Blob with the given file extension via a temporary anchor.
symbols.description: Creates an object URL from the provided blob, sets the download attribute with the artboard name and extension, programmatically clicks the anchor, and revokes the URL after a short delay.

symbols.does: Renders the per-artboard header and card, handling reorder drag, inline rename, menu actions, and focus.
symbols.description: Displays the grip, editable label, kebab menu, and expand button above the artboard card; implements live drag-reorder against sibling slots, two-click delete confirmation, and PNG/HTML export dispatch.

symbols.does: Pointerdown listener that closes the artboard kebab menu when clicking outside.
symbols.description: Registered while the menu is open; if the event target is outside the menu ref, it closes the menu and resets the delete confirmation state.

symbols.does: Menu action that normalizes the artboard name and invokes dcExport for PNG or HTML.
symbols.description: Closes the menu, derives a safe filename from the label or id, and calls dcExport with the card DOM node, dimensions, name, and requested kind, logging errors to the console.

symbols.does: Initiates drag-reorder by capturing pointer events and computing peer slot positions.
symbols.description: Prevents default on grip pointerdown, finds sibling artboard slots in the same section, records their screen positions and the current viewport scale, and adds the dragging class to the active slot.

symbols.does: Applies transform offsets to sibling artboard slots to animate them into their live order during drag.
symbols.description: For every peer except the dragged card, calculates the difference between its current screen position and the slot position implied by the live order, then sets a translateX transform to slide it into place.

symbols.does: Pointermove handler that translates the dragged card and updates live order based on nearest slot.
symbols.description: Moves the dragged card by the pointer delta scaled to local coordinates, determines the nearest slot by screen X, and rebuilds liveOrder when the insertion point changes, triggering layout updates.

symbols.does: Pointerup handler that finalizes drag animation and commits the new order.
symbols.description: Removes listeners, positions the dragged card at its final slot with a transform, waits for the transition to finish, clears transforms, and calls onReorder only if the order actually changed.

symbols.does: ContentEditable inline editor that commits changes on blur or Enter.
symbols.description: Renders a contentEditable element of a configurable tag, stops pointer propagation so clicks do not drag the canvas, and invokes onChange with the textContent when blurred or when Enter is pressed.

symbols.does: Portal-based fullscreen overlay for a focused artboard with keyboard navigation and section switching.
symbols.description: Renders a fixed-position overlay to document.body, scales the focused artboard to fit the viewport, provides left/right arrows and dot navigation within the section, up/down arrows across sections, a section dropdown, and closes on backdrop click or Escape.

symbols.does: Keyboard navigation helper moving focus to the previous or next artboard within the same section.
symbols.description: Wraps around the peer list using modulo arithmetic and calls setFocus with the target section/artboard id when an arrow-left or arrow-right key is pressed.

symbols.does: Keyboard navigation helper moving focus to the first artboard of the next or previous non-empty section.
symbols.description: Steps through sectionOrder in the requested direction, skipping sections whose artboards have all been deleted, and focuses the first artboard of the first non-empty section found.

symbols.does: Keydown listener inside the focus overlay that maps arrow keys to navigation helpers.
symbols.description: Intercepts ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, ArrowUp, and ArrowDown events, preventing default and delegating to go or goSection to move the focused artboard.

symbols.does: Inline button component for left/right focus navigation arrows.
symbols.description: Renders a circular arrow button positioned at the left or right edge of the overlay, calling the provided onClick handler while stopping propagation so the overlay does not close.

symbols.does: Renders an absolute-positioned sticky note with rotation and a handwritten-style font.
symbols.description: A simple presentational component that places a yellow rectangular note at a specified top/left/right/bottom position, applies a slight rotation, and uses a comic/marker font stack for an informal annotation look.
